mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales, as indicated by RSAFS, signify strong consumer demand and economic health, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary environment. This reduces the cost of capital and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (p-value > 0.1) during periods meeting the low inflation and low FEDFUNDS criteria.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, an acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) signals robust investment and future economic activity. This positive sentiment and expected growth translate into short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST acceleration does not show a positive correlation with SPX 5-day returns during periods when GDPC1 growth is above its 5-year average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity. This indicates weakening economic fundamentals that will negatively impact S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances following a two-month consecutive decline in INDPRO greater than 1% each month.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer confidence. This provides a positive backdrop for short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth does not show a statistically significant positive correlation with SPX 5-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a tightening monetary policy. This increases discount rates and negatively impacts corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 4% and FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25% in 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4% YoY and FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.25% in the last 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a weakening economy and potential recession. This leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth rate falls below 0%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when GDPC1 year-over-year growth falls below 0%.
